title: pachycladon exilis
intro: Pachycladon exilis, also known as the "New Zealand liverwort", is a rare, endangered plant species native to New Zealand. It is a type of liverwort, a non-vascular plant that belongs to the class Jungermanniopsida, and has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties. Pachycladon exilis has been found to have potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and capsules, although its use is often limited due to its rarity and the need for proper regulation and handling.
